Document:
# Novakin
Novakin maintain a nitrogen reserve and a fuel-fed Core. Nitrogen always leaks into the local atmosphere; a full intact reserve lasts about thirty minutes. Nitrogen inhalers refill it; other gases do not.
Below 50% reserve, nitrogen loss causes blood-loss and cold damage that worsens as the reserve falls. Once empty, this failure damage stops, but ordinary hazards still affect the inert shell. They have no bloodstream: organic food and alcohol are processed by the Core. Alcohol is an especially energetic fuel that heats the shell. Fuel keeps the core warm; an empty fuel reserve stops thermal regulation.
Temperatures below 323.15 K (50 C) or at or above 700 K make the shell brittle or molten. Both states cause escalating brute and cold/heat damage and rapidly leak nitrogen. A shell also fails once collective brute damage reaches 100.
Novakin are vulnerable to blunt, slash, piercing, and caustic damage; they are immune to shock, poison, and asphyxiation, and especially vulnerable to radiation, cellular, and blood-loss damage. Their glow grows with Core temperature, and their night vision enhances a 120 degree arc ahead of them with a small local fill.
# Survival equipment
# Core restart Dead Novakin cannot be defibrillated. Repair physical shell damage below 100 and total damage below 200, restore at least 50% nitrogen and 10% Core Fuel, then use a lit welder on the dormant Core. The restart takes three seconds and returns them to critical condition.
